programmes.show_description: The story of 90s rave and beyond through the voices of 3 iconic female DJs and producers.
6 Music takes you to the dark side with a playlist featuring the finest goth, electronic and dark pop music, with tracks from Fields of the Nephilim, MIA, UNKLE and Ethel Cain.

programmes.show_name: Indie Forever Disco with Beth Ditto
programmes.show_description: Bringing the club vibes to you - wherever you are, whenever you want. Upbeat music to transport you to the dance floor from Everything Everything, Glass Candy and Hot Chip.
A two-hour mix of ambient music curated by Dr Alex Paterson, aka The Orb, to help you focus your mind and get into the zone. Featuring music by Brian Eno, Teebs and Oval.
Ease into your Saturday with AFRODEUTSCHE. The ultimate soft-launch for your weekend - 2 hours of fluid, deep-listening tracks designed to ground you before the day truly begins.
Mark and Stuart present a feast of great music and comedian Sam Campbell shares his 'First, Last and Everything' and chats about the new series of 'Last One Laughing.'.
Deb Grant sets the weekend vibe with a magical melting pot of amazing music, seamlessly crossing genres, from hip-hop to soul to electronica to rock to dub and more.
Jamz catches up with percussionist and composer Valentina Magaletti to talk about rhythm as language, pushing percussion beyond convention, and her genre-blurring approach.
Gilles chats with Liv.e and Karriem Riggins, aka GENA and pulls out a few exclusive session tracks from the archives. Plus, words from Sparklmami and Nightmares On Wax.

programmes.show_name: The Craig Charles Funk and Soul Show
programmes.show_description: This week's Trunk of Funk comes from DJ and producer Umbo, a long-time figure in the modern funk and breaks scene.
Don's Saturday night selections cross time, space and genre. This week, his record box contains Black Samurai, James Blake, Suzanne Vega, Fauna and Skip Marley amongst others.
SHERELLE's got a bag of summery UK garage, bruk and more as she manifests warmer nights!
One of the UK's most exciting emerging producers jumps into the Sweatbox with half an hour of warm, luscious club music.
Gearing up for this year's 6 Music Festival with dance highlights from previous years.
Feel like a new person with this hand-selected two hour mix featuring Tame Impala, Jorja Smith and Black Sabbath.
As part of 6 Music's All Queens Day, The Last Dinner Party curate two hours of chilled tracks featuring some of their favourite women in music.
Sundays are for staying under the covers a little longer. Let AFRODEUTSCHE provide the perfect, International Women's Day soundtrack.
Mark and Stuart celebrate International Women's Day with an All Queens tracklist including music from Goldfrapp, The Marvelettes, Lykke Li, Yeah Yeah Yeahs and more.
For All Queens Day, Cerys welcomes Camilla George on composition, Jesca Hoop in session, and archive gems from Delia Derbyshire and Daphne Oram, honouring pioneers of women's sound.
Guy talks to singer-songwriter Katherine Priddy. and fills the show with some of his favourite female voices.
Joan as Police Woman sits in for Iggy on International Women's Day. She's got an All Queens Playlist including Nina Simone, Aldous Harding, The Breeders, Low and Little Simz.
Ahead of the release of her third solo album PLAY ME, and on International Women's Day, Music and art icon Kim Gordon is in conversation with Mary Anne.
Stuart marks International Women's Day with an all-female playlist of experimental and avant-garde music.
In honour of All Queens Day, Zakia Sewell celebrates cosmic harp player, Alice Coltrane, via a two-hour deep dive into her life and music.
